policy.title: RBI Releases Final Guidelines on Climate-Related Financial Risk Management for Banks
policy.description: RBI mandates all scheduled commercial banks to integrate climate scenarios into ICAAP by March 2027, with physical risk PD adjustments required for exposures exceeding ₹100 crore. Banks must establish board-level climate risk committees and submit quarterly climate risk dashboards starting Q2 FY27.

policy.title: SEBI Extends BRSR Core Mandatory Assurance to Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI circular expands reasonable assurance requirements for BRSR Core metrics to top 500 companies by market cap from FY27, up from previous top 150 threshold. New metrics include Scope 3 upstream emissions and water stress area disclosures.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Issues Master Circular on Index-Based Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI permits general insurers to offer parametric covers for 12 defined climate perils including flood depth, cyclone windspeed, and heat index triggers. Circular specifies minimum correlation requirements of 0.7 between index and actual loss for product approval.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS publishes final standard requiring internationally active banks to disclose climate-adjusted credit risk metrics by January 2028. Framework mandates granular reporting of physical risk concentrations by geography and sector, with specific templates for transition risk in high-emitting portfolios.

policy.title: ISSB Releases Guidance on Proportionate Application of IFRS S2 for Emerging Markets
policy.description: IFRS Foundation issues implementation guidance allowing phased Scope 3 reporting for companies in emerging market jurisdictions, with 3-year transition relief for physical risk scenario analysis. Guidance specifically addresses data availability challenges in South Asia and Africa.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: Updated NGFS scenarios include sub-national physical risk projections for 15 emerging market countries including India, with district-level acute hazard frequencies. New chronic risk variables cover agricultural yield stress and labor productivity losses from heat exposure.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announces strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to provide ClimateScore Financial for commercial real estate and infrastructure lending portfolios. Deal reportedly valued at $4.5M over three years with exclusive rights for retail lending expansion.

competitors.title: Sprih Raises $12M Series B for ESG SaaS Platform Expansion
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closes Series B led by Jungle Ventures with participation from Accel, bringing total funding to $18M. Company plans to expand BRSR automation suite and enter Southeast Asian markets by Q3 2026.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India Physical Risk Scores with Taluk-Level Resolution
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions releases India-specific physical risk dataset covering 5,900+ taluks with hazard scores for flood, cyclone, drought, and heat stress. Product integrates with Moody's credit risk models and targets Indian bank lending portfolios.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Signs Multi-Year Deal with SBI for Agri-Climate Risk Scoring
competitors.description: CarbonMint partners with State Bank of India to deploy parametric climate risk indices for agricultural lending in 8 states. Platform will provide automated crop-weather correlation scores to support Kisan Credit Card underwriting decisions.

competitors.title: XDI Releases APAC Banking Module with RBI Scenario Alignment
competitors.description: Australia-based XDI launches dedicated banking product for Asia-Pacific covering asset-level physical risk for 14 countries. Module includes pre-configured RBI and MAS climate scenario alignments with portfolio aggregation dashboards.

competitors.title: Watershed Raises $150M Series D, Announces Financial Services Module
competitors.description: Watershed closes Series D at $3.5B valuation, announces dedicated module for financial institutions covering financed emissions calculation and PCAF-aligned portfolio analytics. Company signals emerging markets expansion with India office planned for 2026.

research.title: Climate Risk and Indian Banking: Physical Risk Stress Testing Framework
research.description: RBI working paper finds 12-18% potential increase in NPAs for flood-exposed agricultural portfolios under RCP 8.5 by 2035. Study recommends sector-specific PD multipliers ranging from 1.15x for services to 1.45x for agriculture in high-hazard districts.

research.title: Parametric Solutions for Climate Risk Transfer in Emerging Asia
research.description: Report estimates $14B annual protection gap for climate perils in India, with parametric products potentially covering 35% of uninsured agricultural and SME exposures. Analysis identifies flood and cyclone windspeed indices as highest correlation triggers for South Asian deployments.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Finance
research.description: IFC study finds only 23% of listed companies in South Asia provide decision-useful climate data, compared to 67% in OECD markets. Report recommends regulatory incentives for third-party ESG data providers and standardized emerging market disclosure taxonomies.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment: Methodologies for Credit Risk Integration
research.description: UNEPFI guidance document outlines five approaches for translating hazard data into LGD adjustments, with case studies from 8 pilot banks including ICICI and Axis Bank. Framework proposes collateral value haircuts of 5-25% for assets in high physical risk zones.

research.title: Index Insurance and Financial Inclusion: Evidence from Climate Adaptation Programs
research.description: World Bank evaluation of 23 parametric insurance pilots finds 40% reduction in distress asset sales among covered smallholders in India and Kenya. Study identifies basis risk thresholds below 15% as critical for farmer adoption and product sustainability.

research.title: Climate Risk in Indian Corporate Portfolios: Sectoral Vulnerability Assessment
research.description: CRISIL analysis of 800 rated corporates identifies ₹4.2 lakh crore debt exposure in high physical risk sectors, with coastal infrastructure and thermal power most vulnerable. Report projects 2-3 notch rating pressure for 15% of assessed portfolio by 2030 under moderate warming scenarios.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate risk guidelines create an immediate compliance market while global players race to capture Indian banking relationships—StepChange must move fast on bank partnerships before the window closes.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: Indian climate risk for banks has shifted from voluntary exploration to regulatory mandate. RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's expanded BRSR assurance requirements, and IRDAI's parametric insurance framework collectively create a compliance-driven market that will accelerate procurement cycles across the financial sector. The question is no longer whether Indian FIs will buy climate risk intelligence, but from whom.

The RBI guidelines (policy-1) are transformational for StepChange's core bet. The ₹100 crore exposure threshold for physical risk PD adjustments and mandatory quarterly dashboards create immediate demand for exactly what we build. However, the March 2027 ICAAP deadline means banks will make vendor decisions in the next 6-9 months. The RBI's own working paper (research-1) providing specific PD multiplier recommendations (1.15x-1.45x by sector) is a gift—we should align our models to these ranges and position ourselves as the RBI-compatible solution.

The competitor signals are alarming. Jupiter's HDFC partnership (comp-1) and Moody's taluk-level product launch (comp-3) represent direct incursions into our target market. CarbonMint's SBI deal (comp-4) is particularly concerning—it validates demand for parametric climate indices in agricultural lending but captures a marquee relationship. XDI's RBI-aligned module (comp-5) shows global players are building India-specific products, not just localizing global offerings. We have a differentiation window on proprietary Indian data and regulatory intimacy, but it's narrowing.

The Swiss Re parametric report (research-2) and IRDAI's new master circular (policy-3) together unlock our second strategic bet. The $14B protection gap estimate and specific guidance on 0.7 correlation thresholds give us a product design framework and market sizing in one package. The UNEPFI guidance (research-4) with ICICI and Axis Bank case studies means we can reference credible methodology while the IFC data gaps report (research-3) validates our Global South data thesis—23% disclosure rates in South Asia mean proprietary data providers will command premium positioning.

Watch: Watershed's announced India office (comp-6) and their new FI module signal that well-funded global ESG platforms are coming for the financed emissions space, which could create competitive pressure on our ESG data offering. Action: Accelerate outreach to 3-5 mid-tier banks (private sector, not yet captured by Jupiter/Moody's) with a 'RBI-ready' positioning for PD/LGD integration, using the RBI working paper multipliers as calibration anchors. The compliance clock is ticking.
